text: When working on a software project with a team, Git branching helps organize changes. Developers create feature branches from the main codebase to work on new functions or fixes without disturbing the stable version. For example, you might make a branch called "add-login" to build a login page. After completing and testing your changes, you create a pull request to propose merging your branch with the main branch. Teammates review the code, suggest improvements, and approve it. Then, the changes are merged. This workflow keeps the main code clean and allows multiple people to work simultaneously, making version control efficient and safe.
